chatbot.title: AI Voter Coach
chatbot.subtitle: Powered by Google Gemini
chatbot.welcome: Namaste! I am your AI Voter Coach, powered by Google Gemini. Ask me anything about voting, elections, your rights, or how EVMs work. How can I help you today?
chatbot.error_message: Sorry, I am facing technical issues connecting to the AI. Please try again in a moment.

footer.emergency: Emergency: Call 1950 (Voter Helpline) | Report violations: cVIGIL App | Booth violence: Call 100

rights.title: Voter Laws & Rights
rights.description: The Constitution of India empowers you. Know your legal protections against fraud, coercion, and your rights inside the polling booth.
rights.mcc_title: Witness a Code of Conduct Violation?
rights.mcc_desc: Model Code of Conduct (MCC) violations include distribution of liquor/money, hate speech, or campaigning within 100 meters of a booth.

timeline.title: Election Timeline
timeline.subtitle: The six key phases of every Indian election.
timeline.reg_desc: Electoral rolls are updated. New voters register.
timeline.sil_desc: All campaigning must stop 48 hours before polling.
timeline.res_desc: Votes are counted under ECI supervision.

faq.section_subtitle: Everything you need to know about the electoral process.

min_age.q: What is the minimum age to vote in India?
min_age.a: You must be 18 years of age on the qualifying date (January 1st) to be eligible to vote.

docs.q: What documents can I use at the polling booth?
docs.a: You can use any of these 12 ECI-accepted documents: Voter ID (EPIC), Aadhaar card, passport, driving licence, PAN card, MNREGA job card, etc.

missing_name.q: What if my name is missing from the electoral roll?
missing_name.a: Visit voters.eci.gov.in or apply online. You can also call 1950 for guidance. Names can be added through Form 6.

nota.q: What is NOTA and how does it work?
nota.a: NOTA (None of the Above) lets you reject all candidates. It is counted separately and does not benefit any party.

evm_safe.a: Yes. EVMs are standalone machines with no wired or wireless connectivity. They cannot be remotely accessed.

booth_locator.q: How do I find my polling booth?
booth_locator.a: Use the Poll Booth Locator, visit voters.eci.gov.in, or call 1950. Search by name, EPIC number, or PIN code.

moved_city.q: Can I vote if I have moved to a different city?
moved_city.a: You must vote where you are registered. If moved, update your registration using Form 8A at voters.eci.gov.in.

mcc.q: What is the Model Code of Conduct?
mcc.a: The MCC governs the conduct of parties and candidates from election announcement until results. Report violations via cVIGIL.

nri_vote.q: Can NRIs vote in Indian elections?
nri_vote.a: Yes. Registered overseas voters can vote in person at their constituency polling booth.

bribery.q: What happens if someone offers me money to vote?
bribery.a: Accepting money is a criminal offence. Report immediately to 1950 or via cVIGIL app.

denied_entry.q: What are my rights if I am denied entry to the booth?
denied_entry.a: If your name is on the roll, you cannot be denied. Ask for the Presiding Officer and call 1950.

booth_agent.q: What does a booth agent do?
booth_agent.a: Booth agents observe the process to ensure fairness. They can raise objections but cannot influence voters.

protection.types_subtitle: Understanding the different democratic layers of India.
protection.ls_desc: The Lower House of Parliament. Citizens directly elect Members of Parliament (MPs) every 5 years.
protection.rs_desc: The Upper House. Members are indirectly elected by State Assemblies for 6-year terms.
protection.vs_desc: State Legislative Assembly. Citizens directly elect MLAs to represent their state.
protection.lb_desc: Elections for Panchayats and Municipalities that govern your immediate neighborhood.
protection.laws_subtitle: The statutes that keep our democracy fair and transparent.
protection.rp1950_desc: Governs the registration of voters, allocation of seats, and delimitation of constituencies.
protection.rp1951_desc: The core law detailing the conduct of elections, qualifications of candidates, and corrupt practices.
protection.rules1961_desc: Specifies the technical procedures for voting, including the use of EVMs and VVPATs.
protection.rights_subtitle: Every voter in India is shielded by these fundamental protections.
protection.secret_ballot_desc: Your vote is your secret. No one can legally force you to disclose who you voted for.
protection.rti_desc: You have the right to know your candidate's criminal records, financial assets, and education.
protection.tendered_desc: If someone else has already voted in your name, you can still cast your vote via a physical ballot paper.
protection.nota_desc: The right to reject all candidates in your constituency if you find none of them suitable.

guide.title: First-Time Voter Guide
guide.subtitle: Your roadmap to obtaining a Voter ID and becoming a part of the world's largest democracy.
guide.voter_id_a: The Election Commission of India (ECI) issues a Voter ID (EPIC Card) which allows Indian citizens to vote in elections.
guide.eligibility_subtitle: Ensure you meet these requirements before applying.
guide.docs_options_address: Ration Card, Bill, Passbook, or Rental Agreement
guide.approval_desc: Get EPIC number, download e-EPIC, and receive physical card.
